posts.name: Erdem Yigit Ertorer
posts.text:
Dear combine experts,
Is it possible to exclude certain parameters when running combineTool.py? If so, how can I do that? I checked the combine documentation but didn’t see instructions on how to do so.
Thank you for your help,
Erdem
posts.cooked_html:
Dear combine experts,

Is it possible to exclude certain parameters when running combineTool.py? If so, how can I do that? I checked the combine documentation but didn’t see instructions on how to do so.

Thank you for your help,
Erdem

posts.name: Erdem Yigit Ertorer
posts.text: To be more specific, I want to exclude parameters when running impacts. I am trying to find a way not to create condor jobs for a subset of parameters when running impacts with the --doFits and the --job-mode condor options.
posts.cooked_html: To be more specific, I want to exclude parameters when running impacts. I am trying to find a way not to create condor jobs for a subset of parameters when running impacts with the --doFits and the --job-mode condor options.

posts.name: Andrea Carlo Marini
posts.text:
Hi,
Did you already try --exclude?
$combineTool.py -M Impacts --help
[...]
Impacts options:
options specific to this method
[...]
--exclude PARAM1,PARAM2,...
Skip these nuisances. Also accepts regexp with syntax 'rgx{<my regexp>}'
[...]

-A
posts.cooked_html:
Hi,

Did you already try --exclude?

$combineTool.py -M Impacts --help
[...]
Impacts options:
  options specific to this method
[...]
  --exclude PARAM1,PARAM2,...
                        Skip these nuisances. Also accepts regexp with syntax 'rgx{<my regexp>}'
[...]

-A
